no unit conversion or rounding specified."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"SELECT
  c.code AS course_code,
  c.name AS course_name,
  AVG(ce.workload_hours) AS avg_workload_hours,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS evaluation_count
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
WHERE ce.workload_hours IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
HAVING COUNT(ce.id) >= 1
ORDER BY avg_workload_hours DESC, evaluation_count DESC, course_code ASC
LIMIT 10;","validationIssues":["Vega-Lite x-axis sort is set to sort by -y only, which does not preserve the SQL tie-breaker ordering (evaluation_count DESC, course_code ASC). visualization.title: Top 10 courses by average reported workload (hours)

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT
  c.code AS course_code,
  c.name AS course_name,
  AVG(ce.workload_hours) AS avg_workload_hours,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS evaluation_count
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
WHERE ce.workload_hours IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
HAVING COUNT(ce.id) >= 1
ORDER BY avg_workload_hours DESC, evaluation_count DESC, course_code ASC
LIMIT 10;

issues: Vega-Lite x-axis sort is set to sort by -y only, which does not preserve the SQL tie-breaker ordering (evaluation_count DESC, course_code ASC). The visual order may differ when avg_workload_hours are tied.
Color is mapped to evaluation_count but the chart title/axis do not explain this additional encoding; viewers might misinterpret the color scale without a clearer legend title or explanation.
Course codes on a nominal x-axis can overlap if long; labelAngle=-40 helps, but consider using course_name or increasing rotation/width for readability.

visualization.title: Top 10 courses by average reported workload (hours)

result.sql:
SELECT
  c.code AS course_code,
  c.name AS course_name,
  AVG(ce.workload_hours) AS avg_workload_hours,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS evaluation_count
FROM public.course_evaluations ce
JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses c ON cs.course_id = c.id
WHERE ce.workload_hours IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
HAVING COUNT(ce.id) >= 1
ORDER BY avg_workload_hours DESC, evaluation_count DESC, course_code ASC
LIMIT 10;

issues: Vega-Lite x-axis sort is set to sort by -y only, which does not preserve the SQL tie-breaker ordering (evaluation_count DESC, course_code ASC). The visual order may differ when avg_workload_hours are tied.
Color is mapped to evaluation_count but the chart title/axis do not explain this additional encoding; viewers might misinterpret the color scale without a clearer legend title or explanation.
Course codes on a nominal x-axis can overlap if long; labelAngle=-40 helps, but consider using course_name or increasing rotation/width for readability.
